claim: Hidden checks provide meaningful anti-gaming evidence for accepted tasks, with semantic hidden-pin failures on mutable-definition tasks and signature/downstream guards on proof-only fixed-statement tasks.
primary_evidence: 4/6 accepted tasks have at least one wrong submission reaching hidden pins; 4/4 mutable-definition accepted tasks have hidden-pin wrong failures; 2 proof-only accepted tasks are structurally infeasible for same-signature wrong proofs; 4/6 accepted tasks have semantic positive/negative guards; `pin_coverage_audit`: supported; pin_coverage rows: 26; accepted rows: 6; accepted hidden-pin wrong failures: 5; mutable accepted rows with hidden-pin failures: 4/4; proof-only fixed-statement rows: 2; report exists: True.; `semantic_formalization_pins`: supported; Accepted informal-spec rows in difficulty audit: 1; semantic pin rows: 1.
counterevidence_or_limits: Proof-only fixed-statement rows do not have semantic hidden wrongs because Lean compilation already certifies exact same-signature theorem proofs; hidden pins remain finite probes.
stronger_claim_requires: Add independent reviewer assessment of hidden pins and strengthen any future mutable accepted task until it has at least one public-compiling wrong that fails hidden pins.
